merger_description:
The Michelin Group is a multinational tyre manufacturer and distributor headquartered in Clermont-Ferrand in the Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes region of France. The Michelin Group is the owner of UK-based Fenner PLC, which includes Fenner America, Inc. Fenner is a manufacturer of engineered products, specifically conveyor solutions and polymer products.

Globally, the Michelin Group's activities fall into tyres, polymer composite solutions, data and AI based services for professional mobility and transport operators, and the provision of curated travel, dining and hospitality guides in printed and digital format (including the Michelin Guide). As part of the Michelin Group's polymer composite solutions, it manufactures and sells hydraulic seals that are engineered for dynamic sealing applications (i.e. the sealing between moving parts such as pistons and rods). Customers in this area are predominantly hydraulic related, with products being used in the manufacture and repair of hydraulic cylinders across a range of industries.

Flexitallic is a global manufacturer and supplier of high-performance sealing solutions that are engineered for static sealing applications (i.e. the sealing between stationary parts such as flanged pipe joints and similar joints that do not move or rotate), specifically spiral wound gaskets, flat gaskets and related materials. Flexitallic's products are used in stationary pipeline connections and joints unrelated to cylinders. Flexitallic is headquartered in Paris, France, and has a large global network.

Flexitallic established a local presence through the acquisition of Novus Sealing in 2022. In Australia, Flexitallic focuses on serving the oil and gas, mining, petrochemical, and power generation industries.

Michelin is proposing to acquire 100% of the issued shares in Flexitallic through two steps:

* First, Fenner America, Inc. will acquire 100% of the shares in FGI Acquisition Corp. from The Flexitallic Group SAS.
* Second, Compagnie Financiere Michelin SAS will acquire 100% of the shares in the The Flexitallic Group SAS from Flexitallic, S.à r.l., the parent of the The Flexitallic Group SAS.

Together, these steps will result in Michelin acquiring full ownership of the Flexitallic.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Compagnie Financière Michelin SAS (Michelin) proposes to acquire
100% of the share capital in The Flexitallic Group SAS (Flexitallic),
through the following two steps, as described in the transaction
documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).
Specifically, the Acquisition comprises the following steps:
• First, Fenner America, Inc. (Fenner) (a wholly owned entity of
Michelin) will acquire 100% of the shares in FGI Acquisition
Corp. (a wholly owned entity of Flexitallic).
• Second, Michelin will acquire 100% of the shares in
Flexitallic from Flexitallic, S.à r.l., the parent of Flexitallic.
The Acquisition will result in the Michelin Group acquiring full
ownership of Flexitallic.
Michelin and Fenner applied for a notification waiver in respect of
the Acquisition.

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.

The acquirers, Michelin and Fenner, are part of the Michelin Group.
The Michelin Group is a multinational tyre manufacturer and
distributor headquartered in Clermont-Ferrand in the Auvergne-
Rhône-Alpes region of France. Fenner is a manufacturer of
engineered products, specifically conveyor solutions and polymer
products.
Globally, the Michelin Group's activities include fall into the following
four areas: tyres; polymer composite solutions (development of
polymer composite solutions and other high-tech materials for
critical applications in industrial applications, mobility, construction,
aeronautics, energy, healthcare and mining; connected solutions
(provision of data and AI based services for professional mobility
and transport operators); and lifestyle (provision of curated travel,
dining and hospitality guides in printed and digital format).
As part of the Michelin Group's ‘polymer composite solutions’, it
manufactures and sells hydraulic seals that are engineered for
dynamic sealing applications (i.e. the sealing between moving parts
such as pistons and rods). In Australia, Michelin supplies sealing
solutions via Hallite Seals Australia.
The target, Flexitallic, is a global manufacturer and supplier of high-
performance sealing solutions that are engineered for static sealing
applications (i.e. the sealing between stationary parts such as
flanged pipe joints and similar joints that do not move or rotate),
specifically spiral wound gaskets, flat gaskets and related materials.
Flexitallic's products are used in stationary pipeline connections and
joints unrelated to cylinders. Flexitallic is headquartered in Paris,
France and has a large global network.
In Australia, Flexitallic focuses on serving the oil and gas, mining,
petrochemical, and power generation industries.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition
and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC
considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material
lessening of competition. In particular:
a. there is a small horizontal overlap between Michelin and
Flexitallic in the manufacture and supply of sealing solutions
in Australia
b. the presence of alternative manufacturers and suppliers of
sealing solutions in Australia.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.
